The current visit record primarily details diagnoses and management related to neurological issues (Subdural hemorrhage, Unspecified fall, Incision of cerebral meninges), cardiac conditions (Other primary cardiomyopathies, Atrial fibrillation, Coronary atherosclerosis, Old myocardial infarction, Automatic implantable cardiac defibrillator in situ, Long-term use of anticoagulants, Unspecified essential hypertension), and respiratory issues (Chronic airway obstruction).

While the patient is prescribed Ezetimibe (a lipid-lowering agent), the record does not explicitly state a diagnosis of hypercholesterolemia, hyperlipidemia, or any other primary lipid disorder that would strongly and explicitly warrant a future diagnosis of "Disorders of Lipid Metabolism" based on the strict criteria provided. The presence of Ezetimibe alone, without a corresponding explicit lipid diagnosis, is insufficient evidence to predict a future diagnosis of a primary lipid disorder.
